items.question: namedtuple vs dataclass — how do you choose?
items.answer: Use namedtuple for a tiny, immutable, tuple-like record (indexable, very lightweight). Use a dataclass when you want mutability (or explicit frozen=True), defaults via default_factory, methods, or clearer field semantics.

items.question: Why does defaultdict simplify grouping code?
items.answer: Accessing a missing key triggers the default factory (e.g. list()), so you can append immediately without first checking whether the key exists.

items.question: What breaks if a dataclass field defaults to [] instead of field(default_factory=list)?
items.answer: All instances share the same list object, so appending to one instance's list shows up in every instance — the mutable default bug.
